title: `old_implicit_casting` failed to enable implicit casting for IF function
description:
### What happens?

Before 0.10.0, DuckDB would automatically allow any type to be implicitly cast to VARCHAR during function binding, and clauses like `IF(i > 2, 'text', 0)` were allowed. According to [the doc](https://duckdb.org/docs/1.2/configuration/pragmas.html#implicit-casting-to-varchar), `old_implicit_casting` can be used to enable the implicit casting behavior after 0.10.0. However, this seems to be not true as conversion error will still be raised for `IF` function returning mixed type data. 

### To Reproduce

```
SET old_implicit_casting = true;

CREATE TABLE my_table (
    is_active BOOLEAN,
    value INT
);

-- Insert some sample data
INSERT INTO my_table VALUES 
    (true, 10),
    (false, 20),
    (true, 30),
    (false, 40);

SELECT IF(is_active, 1, 'not_active') FROM my_table;
```

`Conversion Error: Could not convert string 'not_active' to INT32` will be raised

### OS:

shell

### DuckDB Version:

1.3.0

### DuckDB Client:

https://shell.duckdb.org/

### Hardware:

_No response_

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, status BOOLEAN, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, true, 'active'), (2, false, 'inactive'), (3, true, 'running');
SELECT IF(status, 'Status is active', CAST(0 AS CHAR)) FROM test_table;
SELECT IF(status, 'Active', NULL) AS result FROM test_table;
SELECT IF(id > 1, 'Greater than one', 'One or less') FROM test_table;
key_bug_features: Implicit casting failure in IF function with mixed types
